danger_signals.visual_form: Grid-based tactical positioning on battle map
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy robots positioned within attack range represent immediate threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Red or contrasting unit coloration for enemy forces
danger_signals.meaning: Visual distinction of enemy-controlled robots vs. allied units

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger or distinctly animated super robot sprite
danger_signals.meaning: Final boss encounter indicates critical danger/narrative peak

danger_signals.visual_form: Health bar reduction during combat animation

safety_signals.visual_form: Blue or team-colored robot sprites on tactical grid
safety_signals.meaning: Player-controlled or allied forces provide tactical support

safety_signals.visual_form: Full HP bar; enhanced unit visuals post-upgrade
Player units occupying terrain tiles; clear sightlines
safety_signals.meaning: Established position reduces enemy approach angles

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Aura Battler variants; distinctly magical mecha appearance vs. mechanical robots
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fantasy-influenced setting distinct from standard mecha warfare

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Alternative Getter configurations (Getter G); antagonistic unit skins

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Halo/V-fin head crest; monoeye or dual-eye faceplate variants
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Char's forces (red mobile suits); Zeon antagonistic designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military mecha aesthetic; federation vs. principality visual conflict

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Giant humanoid super robot form; distinctive head design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Great Mazinger (upgraded appearance); antagonist robot variants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Massive proportions; iconic 1970s mecha design language
Aesthetic distinct from magical (Dunbine) or military (Gundam) factions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fantasy-influenced environmental tileset; magical aura effects around Aura Battler units
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dunbine and Aura Battler units display magical attack animations; scenario branching based on magical threat level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: UI presentation of selectable scenario order affects unit roster composition
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different enemy configurations appear based on scenario selection sequence; narrative consequences reflected in ally availability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enhanced robot sprite details; weapon attachments visibly added to mecha frames
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Attack animations expand; damage output increases; new attack options become available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Map density increases with enemy unit count; boss unit appearance signals narrative climax
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy AI aggression increases; multiple faction enemy types appear simultaneously

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hidden items and credit pickups distributed across tactical map as collectible tokens
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must balance combat objectives with resource gathering; map exploration incentivized

environmental_storytelling.example: Gundam series robots appear in military conflict scenarios; Mazinger units in direct superhuman threat scenarios; Dunbine units in fantasy-tinged story moments
Gilgilgan (final boss from Great Mazinger vs. Getter Robo source material) appears with distinct animation or sprite size to signal narrative significance
Golden-age super robots (Mazinger, Getter) occupy same battlefield with UC Gundam mobile suits, creating visual anachronism that reflects series mashup nature
Multiple selectable scenario paths shown in menu structure; player choice directly affects which robot series featured prominently in subsequent maps
Magical/fantasy environments correlate with Masō Kishin (Dunbine, Aura Battler) deployments; urban/industrial terrain correlates with Gundam/Mazinger scenarios

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Active in Masō Kishin plot scenarios
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Deploys with magical attack animations; suggests transformation/magical state narrative
Displays combination/transformation attack sequences; signals dynamic mechanical synergy
Standard mobile suit movement and ballistic attack animations; conveys military protocol
Largest sprite/animation scale; dominates visual battlefield presence
Enhanced health/damage values reflected in prolonged combat animation; higher attack frequency indicates escalated threat
Grid positioning determines tactical approach; proximity to enemy conveys imminent conflict

notes: Super Robot Wars EX is foundational for understanding manifestation in the series due to its 'Multiple Scenario' system—world-state is directly communicated through player choice in scenario order, which branches narrative and roster availability. The game's primary innovation is elevating Masō Kishin (magical/fantasy mecha) to equivalent narrative weight as Gundam/Mazinger (military/technological mecha), creating visual collision that signals genre-blend world-state. The introduction of weapon upgrades means unit visual appearance shifts with progression. Limited battle backgrounds (preceding this in 3rd SRW) mean faction identity and world-state rely heavily on unit sprite, attack animation, and enemy positioning communication. No documented color-coding or faction symbol systems are detailed in available sources; inferences drawn from series-wide conventions.
